# Research: mini-golf-obstacle-taxonomy

*Auto-generated by AIDLC planning session*

**Question:** What is the full taxonomy of mini-golf obstacle types used in real courses and existing mini-golf video games? Categorize into static (walls, bumpers, ramps, funnels), dynamic (windmills, gates, sliders), and hazard (water, OOB, penalty zones) types with their gameplay purposes and placement rules.

---

It looks like the file write is being blocked by permissions. Could you approve the write permission so I can save the document to `docs/research/mini-golf-obstacle-taxonomy.md`?

In the meantime, here's a summary of what the document covers across **8 sections**:

## Document Overview

### 1. Static Obstacles (6 subcategories)
- **Walls** — 6 variants (straight, curved, angled deflector, corner, side rail, center island) with collision thickness rules, gap sizing, and angle guidelines
- **Bumpers** — 5 variants (round, mushroom, bar, diamond, elastic) with spacing rules (3x ball diameter min), restitution values (0.6-1.3), and cluster limits
- **Ramps & Elevation** — 6 variants (incline, decline, bridge, step, tiered platform, launch ramp) with angle ranges (10-40 degrees) and landing zone requirements
- **Funnels/Bowls/Contours** — 6 variants (funnel, bowl, crown, channel, saddle, banked curve) with slope gradients and depth ratios
- **Tunnels & Passages** — 4 variants (straight, curved, split, one-way) with entrance width and length rules
- **Static Gates** — 3 variants (narrow gate, keyhole, goal posts) with gap width ratios (2.5-4x ball diameter)

### 2. Dynamic Obstacles (4 subcategories)
- **Windmills/Rotators** — 5 variants with cycle times (2-10 sec), safe window percentages (30-70%), and the "observe 2 full cycles" rule
- **Dynamic Gates** — 5 variants (timed, alternating, drawbridge, sliding, force field) with open/close durations and telegraph requirements
- **Sliders/Sweepers** — 5 variants (linear slider, pendulum, sweeper arm, conveyor belt, piston) with travel distances and speed ranges
- **Launchers/Transporters** — 4 variants (boost pad, portal, launch tube, elevator) with boost magnitudes and landing zone requirements

### 3. Hazard Obstacles (4 subcategories)
- **Water** — 4 variants with penalty rules (+1 stroke, NOT back to tee)
- **OOB** — 3 variants (edge fall-off, void, gutter) with reset position guidelines
- **Penalty Zones** — 6 variants (sand, sticky, magnetic, bounce, speed boost, reset) with friction multipliers (3-10x)
- **Timed Hazards** — 3 variants (periodic, triggered, expanding) with cycle timing

### 4-5. Hybrid Obstacles & Summary
- 7 named combination patterns with the "max 2 types per challenge point" rule
- Full summary table mapping all 14 obstacle types to skills tested and recommended frequency per 18 holes

### 6. Universal Placement Rules
- 7 named rules: 30-60% coverage, readability, recovery, spacing, escalation, one-new-thing, telegraph

### 7. Project Mapping
- Maps all 12 existing mechanics (`BankWall`, `MovingSweeper`, `PortalGate`, etc.) to their taxonomy positions
- Identifies 7 coverage gaps with priority rankings (Water Hazard and Sand Trap = high priority)
- Full Orbital Drift space-theme visual mapping for all obstacle types

### 8. Sources
- Real-world: WMF, USPMGA, Putt-Putt, Puttshack, PopStroke
- Video games: Golf With Your Friends, Golf It!, Walkabout Mini Golf, Infinite Minigolf, What The Golf?
- Design literature: Schell, Kremers, GDC talks
